## Test File Structure

```
android/src/test/kotlin/org/tele/flutter_dialer/
├── FlutterDialerPluginTest.kt    # Main test class
└── MethodChannelTest.kt          # Method channel tests (optional)
```

---

## Test Class: FlutterDialerPluginTest

### Setup

```kotlin
package org.tele.flutter_dialer

import io.flutter.embedding.engine.plugins.FlutterPlugin
import io.flutter.plugin.common.MethodCall
import io.flutter.plugin.common.MethodChannel
import io.flutter.plugin.common.MethodChannel.Result
import io.mockk.*
import org.junit.Before
import org.junit.Test
import org.junit.Assert.*
import android.content.Context
import android.telecom.TelecomManager
import android.os.Build

class FlutterDialerPluginTest {

    private lateinit var plugin: FlutterDialerPlugin
    private lateinit var mockContext: Context
    private lateinit var mockTelecomManager: TelecomManager
    private lateinit var mockBinding: FlutterPlugin.FlutterPluginBinding
    private lateinit var mockMessenger: BinaryMessenger
    private lateinit var mockResult: Result

    @Before
    fun setUp() {
        plugin = FlutterDialerPlugin()
        mockContext = mockk()
        mockTelecomManager = mockk()
        mockBinding = mockk()
        mockMessenger = mockk()
        mockResult = mockk()

        every { mockBinding.binaryMessenger } returns mockMessenger
        every { mockBinding.applicationContext } returns mockContext
        every { mockContext.getSystemService(Context.TELECOM_SERVICE) } returns mockTelecomManager
    }
}
```

---

## Test Implementations

### Lifecycle Tests

```kotlin
@Test
fun onAttachedToEngine_initializesChannel() {
    // Arrange
    val plugin = FlutterDialerPlugin()

    // Act
    plugin.onAttachedToEngine(mockBinding)

    // Assert
    // Verify channel was created with correct name
    // Verify handler was set
    // (Requires capturing MethodChannel constructor args)
}

@Test
fun onDetachedFromEngine_clearsHandler() {
    // Arrange
    plugin.onAttachedToEngine(mockBinding)

    // Act
    plugin.onDetachedFromEngine(mockBinding)

    // Assert
    // Verify setMethodCallHandler(null) was called
}
```

---

### Method Routing Tests

```kotlin
@Test
fun onMethodCall_routesIsDefaultDialer() {
    // Arrange
    val mockCall = mockk<MethodCall>()
    every { mockCall.method } returns "isDefaultDialer"

    // Act
    plugin.onMethodCall(mockCall, mockResult)

    // Assert
    verify { mockResult.success(any<Boolean>()) }
}

@Test
fun onMethodCall_routesSetDefaultDialer() {
    // Arrange
    val mockCall = mockk<MethodCall>()
    every { mockCall.method } returns "setDefaultDialer"

    // Act
    plugin.onMethodCall(mockCall, mockResult)

    // Assert
    verify { mockResult.success(true) }
}

@Test
fun onMethodCall_routesCanSetDefaultDialer() {
    // Arrange
    val mockCall = mockk<MethodCall>()
    every { mockCall.method } returns "canSetDefaultDialer"

    // Act
    plugin.onMethodCall(mockCall, mockResult)

    // Assert
    verify { mockResult.success(any<Boolean>()) }
}

@Test
fun onMethodCall_unknownMethod_returnsNotImplemented() {
    // Arrange
    val mockCall = mockk<MethodCall>()
    every { mockCall.method } returns "unknownMethod"

    // Act
    plugin.onMethodCall(mockCall, mockResult)

    // Assert
    verify { mockResult.notImplemented() }
}
```

### Version Handling Tests

```kotlin
@Test
fun isDefaultDialer_apiLessThan23_returnsTrue() {
    // Arrange - This requires Robolectric or shadowing Build.VERSION
    // For now, this test may need to be manual or use a different approach

    // Act & Assert would verify:
    // - Returns true without accessing TelecomManager
}

@Test
fun canSetDefaultDialer_apiLessThan23_returnsFalse() {
    // Arrange - Requires Robolectric

    // Act & Assert would verify:
    // - Returns false without accessing TelecomManager
}
```

**Note**: Testing `Build.VERSION.SDK_INT` requires Robolectric or reflection. Consider extracting version check to a testable function.

### Error Handling Tests

```kotlin
@Test
fun isDefaultDialer_exception_returnsError() {
    // Arrange
    every { mockContext.getSystemService(Context.TELECOM_SERVICE) }
        .throws(Exception("Test exception"))

    // Act
    // Need to make method accessible for testing
    // Option: Use reflection or make internal methods public for testing

    // Assert
    verify {
        mockResult.error(
            eq("IS_DEFAULT_DIALER_ERROR"),
            any(),
            any()
        )
    }
}

@Test
fun setDefaultDialer_exception_returnsError() {
    // Arrange
    every { mockContext.startActivity(any()) }
        .throws(SecurityException("Missing permission"))

    // Act & Assert
    // Verify error is returned with correct code
}

@Test
fun canSetDefaultDialer_exception_returnsError() {
    // Arrange
    every { mockContext.getSystemService(Context.TELECOM_SERVICE) }
        .throws(ClassCastException("Invalid cast"))

    // Act & Assert
    // Verify error is returned with correct code
}
```

### Business Logic Tests

```kotlin
@Test
fun isDefaultDialer_samePackage_returnsTrue() {
    // Arrange
    val packageName = "org.tele.flutter_dialer"
    every { mockContext.packageName } returns packageName
    every { mockTelecomManager.defaultDialerPackage } returns packageName

    // Act
    // Call isDefaultDialer via reflection or make accessible

    // Assert
    verify { mockResult.success(true) }
}

@Test
fun isDefaultDialer_differentPackage_returnsFalse() {
    // Arrange
    every { mockContext.packageName } returns "org.tele.flutter_dialer"
    every { mockTelecomManager.defaultDialerPackage } returns "com.other.dialer"

    // Act
    // Call isDefaultDialer

    // Assert
    verify { mockResult.success(false) }
}

@Test
fun canSetDefaultDialer_alreadyDefault_returnsFalse() {
    // Arrange
    val packageName = "org.tele.flutter_dialer"
    every { mockContext.packageName } returns packageName
    every { mockTelecomManager.defaultDialerPackage } returns packageName

    // Act
    // Call canSetDefaultDialer

    // Assert
    verify { mockResult.success(false) }
}

@Test
fun canSetDefaultDialer_notDefault_returnsTrue() {
    // Arrange
    every { mockContext.packageName } returns "org.tele.flutter_dialer"
    every { mockTelecomManager.defaultDialerPackage } returns "com.other.dialer"

    // Act
    // Call canSetDefaultDialer

    // Assert
    verify { mockResult.success(true) }
}

@Test
fun setDefaultDialer_startsActivity() {
    // Arrange
    val packageName = "org.tele.flutter_dialer"
    every { mockContext.packageName } returns packageName
    every { mockContext.startActivity(any()) } just Runs

    // Act
    // Call setDefaultDialer

    // Assert
    verify {
        mockContext.startActivity(capture<Intent>())
    }
    // Verify captured intent has correct action and extras
}
```

## Refactoring for Testability

### Problem: Private Methods

The current implementation has private methods that are hard to test directly.

**Solution 1**: Make methods internal/public for testing

```kotlin
// For testing
internal fun isDefaultDialer(result: Result) { ... }
```

**Solution 2**: Extract logic to testable functions

```kotlin
// Extract pure function
fun checkIsDefaultDialer(
    telecomManager: TelecomManager,
    packageName: String
): Boolean {
    return telecomManager.defaultDialerPackage == packageName
}

// Test the pure function
@Test
fun checkIsDefaultDialer_samePackage_returnsTrue() {
    // Test pure function directly
}
```

### Problem: Static Build.VERSION

**Solution**: Extract version check to injectable dependency

```kotlin
interface VersionChecker {
    fun isAtLeastM(): Boolean
}

class AndroidVersionChecker : VersionChecker {
    override fun isAtLeastM(): Boolean {
        return Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.M
    }
}

// In plugin
private var versionChecker: VersionChecker = AndroidVersionChecker()

// For testing
plugin.versionChecker = mockk<VersionChecker>()
```

---

## Integration Test Structure

```kotlin
// integration_test/dialer_test.dart
import 'package:flutter_test/flutter_test.dart';
import 'package:integration_test/integration_test.dart';

void main() {
  IntegrationTestWidgetsFlutterBinding.ensureInitialized();

  group('FlutterDialer Integration Tests', () {
    testWidgets('isDefaultDialer returns boolean', (tester) async {
      // Test actual platform call
    });

    testWidgets('canSetDefaultDialer returns boolean', (tester) async {
      // Test actual platform call
    });
  });
}
```

---

## Running Tests

### Unit Tests

```bash
cd android
./gradlew test

# With coverage
./gradlew test jacocoTestReport
```

### Integration Tests

```bash
# From project root
flutter test integration_test/

# Or via gradle
cd android
./gradlew connectedAndroidTest
```

---

## Coverage Report

Generate coverage report:

```bash
cd android
./gradlew jacocoTestReport

# View report
open build/reports/jacoco/jacocoTestReport/html/index.html
```

**Target**: >90% line coverage

---

## CI/CD Integration

### GitHub Actions Example

```yaml
name: Tests
on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      
      - name: Set up Flutter
        uses: subosito/flutter-action@v2
        
      - name: Run Flutter tests
        run: flutter test
        
      - name: Run Android unit tests
        run: |
          cd android
          ./gradlew test
          
      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v3
```
